label: Redis
description: Connect to a Redis in-memory key-value store using a connection string or manual host/port credentials.

form.title: Connect Redis
form.subtitle: Paste a connection string or enter host and port details to link Anton to your Redis instance.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste a single redis:// or rediss:// URI — the quickest way to connect. Encodes host, port, password, and database number in one field.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Redis Connection String

A Redis connection string bundles all the connection details into one URI. This is the fastest path.

### URI format

- Unencrypted: `redis://:yourpassword@hostname:6379/0`
- TLS/SSL: `rediss://:yourpassword@hostname:6379/0`
- No password: `redis://hostname:6379/0`
- With username (Redis 6+ ACL): `redis://username:password@hostname:6379/0`

### Steps

1. Find your Redis host, port, and password from your provider's dashboard (e.g. Redis Cloud, Upstash, ElastiCache, or your self-hosted config file `redis.conf`).
2. Assemble the URI following the format above, or copy it directly from your provider's connection panel.
3. If your instance uses TLS (recommended for cloud deployments), use the `rediss://` scheme (double-s).
4. Paste the full URI into the **Connection String** field below and click **Connect**.

### Notes

- The default database index is `0`; change the trailing `/N` to switch databases (e.g. `/2`).
- If no password is set, omit the `:password@` portion entirely.
- For Redis Sentinel or Cluster topologies, use the **Manual** method instead and configure host/port per node.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full Redis URI. Use rediss:// (double-s) for TLS-encrypted connections.

methods.label: Host / Port / Password
methods.description: Enter the host, port, password, and database number individually. Useful when you don't have a pre-formatted URI or need fine-grained control.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Host, Port, and Password

Fill in each connection detail separately. Helpful if your provider gives you individual fields rather than a full URI.

### Steps

1. Locate your Redis host (e.g. `my-instance.cache.amazonaws.com` or `127.0.0.1`) from your provider dashboard or `redis.conf`.
2. Note the port — Redis default is **6379**. Cloud providers sometimes use a different port.
3. Find your password. In self-hosted Redis this is the `requirepass` value in `redis.conf`. In managed services it's shown in the credentials panel.
4. Identify the database number you want to use. Redis ships with 16 databases (0–15); the default is **0**.
5. Enter each value in the fields below and click **Connect**.

### Notes

- If your instance has no password set, leave the **Password** field blank.
- Enable **Use TLS** if your Redis endpoint requires encrypted connections (common with cloud-hosted instances).
- Redis usernames are required only when using ACL-based authentication (Redis 6+). Leave blank if not applicable.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of your Redis server.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: TCP port Redis is listening on. Default is 6379.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Leave blank if your Redis instance has no authentication configured.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Required only for Redis 6+ ACL-based auth. Leave blank if not applicable.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Number
fields.description: Redis logical database index (0–15). Defaults to 0.

fields.name: use_tls
fields.label: Use TLS (SSL)
fields.description: Enable TLS encryption. Required for most cloud-hosted Redis instances.
